$schema.description: Delivery session log for RideCircle Carpool Marketplace

fields.phase: Delivery phase this session belongs to
fields.files_changed: Array of file paths created or modified
fields.open_risks: Array of risks identified or still open
fields.decisions: Array of decision objects with 'what' and 'why' keys

summary: Initial project scaffolding and source-of-truth documentation. Created full directory structure, metadata, design document, API specification, route inventory, screen specification, test coverage strategy, reviewer notes, and session log schema. Backend scaffolded with ThinkPHP 6, middleware, validators, route definitions, exception classes, and helper functions.
requirements_addressed: Architecture design: decoupled ThinkPHP backend + Layui frontend
Auth model: session login + API tokens with 90-day expiry and rotation
RBAC model: Administrator, Moderator, Driver, Regular User
Listing workflow: draft, active, matched, in-progress, completed, canceled, disputed
Order lifecycle: pending-match (30min auto-expire), accepted, in-progress, completed, canceled, disputed
Cancellation rules: free within 5min, reason-code after, blocked in-progress
Dispute window: 72 hours after completion, Administrator resolution required
Review constraints: 1-5 stars, 1000 chars, max 5 files, photo 5MB, video 50MB
Rate limit: max 3 reviews per user per hour
Content moderation: sensitive-word, rate-limit, duplicate detection, credibility score
Media: local disk, signed URLs (10min default), watermark, hotlink protection
Data governance: event capture, nightly dedup, lineage, quality metrics
Offline-first: no external dependencies, local assets only
Search: keyword suggestions, on-device history, did-you-mean, no-results fallback
open_risks: ThinkPHP version selection not yet finalized (targeting 6.x or 8.x)
Layui version must be bundled locally -- need to confirm asset packaging
Text similarity threshold for duplicate detection needs empirical tuning
Credibility score weights need product sign-off
Watermark overlay implementation approach (GD vs Imagick) not yet decided
Nightly job scheduler: ThinkPHP command + cron vs dedicated queue -- TBD

decisions.what: Docs-first delivery: write all design and specification documents before any application code
decisions.why: Enables static review of intent and risk handling before implementation begins

decisions.what: Single MySQL database with organization_id tenant column
decisions.why: Simpler than multi-database for an offline private-community deployment

decisions.what: Local dictionary-based typo correction for did-you-mean
decisions.why: Offline constraint forbids external spell-check or search APIs

decisions.what: SHA-256 file fingerprinting for media duplicate detection
decisions.why: Deterministic, no external dependency, fast on local disk

decisions.what: Signed URLs use HMAC-SHA256 with server secret + expiry timestamp
decisions.why: Standard approach, works fully offline, configurable expiry

notes: Phase 0 covered documentation and backend scaffolding. Middleware, validators, exception classes, route definitions, and config files are all in place. No service or controller implementation yet.
